goal: Land the `diagnostics` sub-module of `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` implementing LSP JSON-RPC client calls for `textDocument/diagnostic`, `callHierarchy/prepareCallHierarchy` + `incomingCalls` + `outgoingCalls`, and `typeHierarchy/supertypes` — dispatched through a new `SerenaClient` trait (per DEC-0008 §4) when Serena is active, and erroring cleanly when Serena is absent (degraded-mode endpoints are reserved for P1-W5-F07).
plan_summary: Per DEC-0008 §4, P1-W5-F04 dispatches LSP requests in two branches: `serena_managed = true` → route through the MCP channel Serena already owns; `serena_managed = false` → would speak JSON-RPC to standalone LSP subprocesses, but F07 has not yet populated the endpoint map. This WO implements the first branch end-to-end and sets up the second branch to return a typed `NoLspServerConfigured` error until F07 lands. New module `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/diagnostics.rs` introduces: (1) LSP request/response types via the community `lsp-types` crate (workspace dep add) — `Diagnostic`, `CallHierarchyItem`, `CallHierarchyIncomingCall`, `CallHierarchyOutgoingCall`, `TypeHierarchyItem`; (2) a `SerenaClient` async trait with four methods (`diagnostics`, `call_hierarchy_incoming`, `call_hierarchy_outgoing`, `type_hierarchy_supertypes`) that forms the dependency-inversion boundary between `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` and the future daemon integration — the trait is UCIL's own abstraction, not a mock of Serena MCP; (3) a `DiagnosticsClient` struct holding `Arc<dyn SerenaClient + Send + Sync>` that exposes the four LSP request methods and honours a `tokio::time::timeout` on every `.await`; (4) extension to `bridge.rs` adding `LspDiagnosticsBridge::with_serena_client(Arc<dyn SerenaClient>) -> Self` as an additive constructor per DEC-0008 §Consequences (does NOT break `new(serena_managed: bool)`), and `LspDiagnosticsBridge::diagnostics_client(&self) -> Option<DiagnosticsClient>` returning `Some` only when the bridge was constructed with a client; (5) a `BridgeError::NoLspServerConfigured` variant for the degraded-mode path pre-F07; (6) module-root tests under `diagnostics::` that use an in-test `FakeSerenaClient` impl (implementing UCIL's own `SerenaClient` trait — not mocking Serena MCP itself) to prove the four dispatch paths wire through correctly, plus a test covering the `NoLspServerConfigured` error branch. All four LSP operations are async and wrap every `.await` in `tokio::time::timeout` using a named const `LSP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000`. No new `ucil-daemon` dependency edge — the trait stays in `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` and the daemon integration WO (future) provides the concrete `SerenaClient` impl that forwards to `PluginManager`.
scope_in: crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/lib.rs — add `pub mod diagnostics;` + re-export `DiagnosticsClient`, `SerenaClient`, `DiagnosticsClientError` (or equivalent error type)

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs — add `pub fn with_serena_client(serena_client: Arc<dyn SerenaClient + Send + Sync>) -> Self` constructor and `pub fn diagnostics_client(&self) -> Option<DiagnosticsClient>` accessor; the existing `new(bool)` constructor stays untouched per DEC-0008
crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs — add `BridgeError::NoLspServerConfigured { language: Language }` variant for the degraded-mode path; `#[non_exhaustive]` already set
crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/Cargo.toml — add `lsp-types = { workspace = true }` (new workspace dep) and `async-trait = { workspace = true }` (new workspace dep for the `SerenaClient` trait)

Four module-root tests under `diagnostics::` exercising the Serena-managed dispatch path via a `FakeSerenaClient`: `test_diagnostics_via_serena`, `test_call_hierarchy_incoming_via_serena`, `test_call_hierarchy_outgoing_via_serena`, `test_type_hierarchy_supertypes_via_serena`
One module-root test `diagnostics::test_no_lsp_configured_returns_error` exercising the degraded-mode branch when bridge is constructed via `new(false)` without a SerenaClient
`FakeSerenaClient` lives in a `#[cfg(test)]` nested submodule inside `diagnostics.rs` as a real implementation of UCIL's own `SerenaClient` trait (not a mock of Serena MCP)
All `.await` in `DiagnosticsClient` dispatch methods wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_millis(LSP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS), ...)`
Rustdoc on every public item: `Arc<dyn SerenaClient>`, `DiagnosticsClient`, each dispatch method, `with_serena_client`, `diagnostics_client`, `NoLspServerConfigured`, `LSP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS`
`#![warn(clippy::all, clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)]` clean on all new code (per existing `lib.rs:16`)
scope_out: Standalone LSP subprocess spawning (pyright, rust-analyzer, tsserver) — reserved for P1-W5-F07
Real `PluginManager`-backed `SerenaClient` implementation — reserved for a future daemon-integration WO; this WO only lands the trait and an in-test fake
Feeding `quality_issues` table from diagnostics — P1-W5-F05 territory
Feeding G4 architecture with call/type hierarchies — P1-W5-F06 territory

Any change to `types.rs` — `Diagnostic`/`DiagnosticSeverity` stay untouched (the new code uses `lsp_types::Diagnostic` directly; an adapter between the two forms is explicitly F05's concern per `types.rs:132-134` docs)

Any `DashMap` promotion of `endpoints` or `diagnostics_cache` — F03 docs say F04 may do this if concurrent access lands; this WO does not require it (single-threaded dispatch through `Arc<dyn SerenaClient>`)
ADRs — DEC-0008 already covers the delegation design; no new ADR needed unless the executor hits a precondition break (e.g. `lsp-types` crate version conflict)
Adding `ucil-daemon` as a dep of `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` — DEC-0008 explicitly forbids this cycle
Any Serena Docker integration test — those run under P1-W5-F08
acceptance: New `diagnostics` module exists at `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/diagnostics.rs` and is re-exported from `lib.rs`
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics diagnostics::` matches ≥ 5 tests, all passing (the four `test_*_via_serena` + `test_no_lsp_configured_returns_error`)
`cargo clippy -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics -- -D warnings` is clean (pedantic + nursery)
`cargo doc -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --no-deps` is clean (no broken intra-doc links)
Reality-check oracle: stashing `diagnostics.rs` causes the ≥5 `diagnostics::` tests to fail (selector misses on stashed state), popping restores them
`LspDiagnosticsBridge::new(bool)` signature is byte-for-byte unchanged from WO-0014 (grep proves it)
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics 'diagnostics::' --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -E '^\s+PASS' | wc -l | awk '{exit ($1 < 5)}'
cargo build -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics 2>&1 | grep -q 'warning' && exit 1 || cargo build -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics
cargo doc -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --no-deps 2>&1 | grep -qE 'warning|error' && exit 1 || cargo doc -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --no-deps
grep -q 'pub fn new(serena_managed: bool) -> Self' crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs
context_refs: master-plan:§13.4 LSP server sharing with Serena (lines 1424-1430)
master-plan:§13.5 How it feeds other groups (lines 1432-1439)
lessons_applied: Module-root tests for the frozen `diagnostics::` selector (WO-0006/WO-0007/WO-0010/WO-0011/WO-0013/WO-0014 lesson); a nested `#[cfg(test)] mod fake_serena_client { ... }` is acceptable ONLY for the test-side `FakeSerenaClient` impl because the frozen selector is a module prefix (`diagnostics::`) — any test at `diagnostics::test_*` matches regardless of whether it is inside a nested mod, but per DEC-0005 the five selector-exercising tests stay at module root

No mocking of Serena MCP (phase-log invariant 1); the `SerenaClient` trait is UCIL's own dependency-inversion seam, and `FakeSerenaClient` implements that UCIL-owned trait — this is structurally distinct from mocking Serena's MCP wire format
Every `.await` wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout` with a named const (rust-style.md Async §)
`thiserror` + `#[non_exhaustive]` on `BridgeError` additions, never `anyhow` in library code (rust-style.md Errors §)

`Arc<dyn SerenaClient + Send + Sync>` pattern chosen over `&dyn SerenaClient` because the bridge needs to hand ownership of the client to `DiagnosticsClient` instances, and `Send + Sync` is required for `tokio::spawn` use in the future daemon-integration WO
`LspDiagnosticsBridge::new(serena_managed: bool)` untouched — DEC-0008 §Consequences freezes it for Phase 1; this WO adds `with_serena_client` as a peer constructor
